summary.category: Science as a Process of Discovery and Curiosity
points: Science is not just facts—it's a way of thinking that welcomes curiosity, asks deeper questions (How do things work? Why do events happen?), and is open to the unknown. Science is an ongoing process of discovery where questions lead to experiments, which lead to more questions.

summary.category: Interconnected Topics: From Materials to Celestial Movements
points: Grade 7 Science covers interconnected fields: properties of materials → electricity and metals/non-metals → reversible and irreversible changes → heat and water cycle → life processes → time measurement → light and shadows → Earth-Moon-Sun movements. Ideas from one field inspire discoveries in another, creating a holistic understanding of science.

Learning happens beyond textbooks through activities and experiments that serve as stepping stones to deeper understanding. Students develop scientific thinking by asking interesting questions (not just answering them), making simple observations, and testing ideas. Even experiments confirming predictions can lead to new questions requiring further investigation.

Young science explorers must understand how human activities link to natural world consequences and societal impact. Science plays a crucial role in addressing environmental challenges like glacier melting and water cycles, helping create a more sustainable world while maintaining the wonder and amazement about our planet.
